Identity.History: Darius III ruled the Achaemenid Persian Empire from 336–330 BC and was its last king. Coming to power amid court turmoil, he faced Alexander the Great’s invasion soon after. Darius fought to defend the empire but was defeated at Issus in 333 BC and Gaugamela in 331 BC, losing control of the Persian heartland. He fled east, hoping to raise another army, but was betrayed and murdered by the satrap Bessus. His death marked the end of Achaemenid rule and the rise of Alexander’s empire.
